rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Aside Or, Alt, Clarify August 13, 2021 Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KATHRYN DEVINCENZI Motion To Aside Or, Alt, Clarify August 13, 2021 Order is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion to set aside any part of August 12, 2021 is DENIED. The Court clarifies that all costs associated with appointment of referee and costs incurred by referee from the date of appointment to the date of entry of judgement for partition is attributed to the Defendant and this should be reflected in the final judgment. The costs and fees incurred by the parties after the entry of judgment for partition shall be born by the respective parties incurring the costs/fees. =501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JUAN SALAS ET AL VS. GALE LEA GRINSELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION To The Second-Amended Complaint Of Pamela Uberti
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT GALE LEA GRINSELL,, FOUNDERS REALTY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION To The Second-Amended Complaint Of Pamela Uberti is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden. The motion for summary judgment/adjudication is framed by the pleadings. While the fire may be the "crux" of Plaintiff's complaint (as asserted in the Notice of Motion) is it not the only basis for the complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JADE CHAPMAN VS. CHOICE HOTELS INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: JADE CHAPMAN VS. CHOICE HOTELS INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT CHOICE HOTELS INTERNATIONAL, INC., COMFORT INN BY THE BAY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of each cause of action. All amendments shall be in bold font. The Court notes that this action is pending in San Francisco, not in Riverside (Opposition 15: 2-4) and Plaintiff shall allege facts in good faith supporting causes of action in this San Francisco case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
